engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Wagon R Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Suzuki Wagon R in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Suzuki Wagon R K6A engine - petrol 0.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - common after 100k km on high-revving 3-cylinder units.
Head gasket failure - MLS gasket upgrade recommended on rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Suzuki Wagon R M13A engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - generally reliable but check at 100k miles.
Head gasket failure - documented on higher-mileage units, MLS upgrade available.

cards.altText: Suzuki Wagon R Z13DT engine - diesel 1.2L replacement UK
productionHistory: GL, GLX, SZ trim levels with diesel option - 2003-2014
knownFailures: Common-rail injector failure - £150-£200 per injector, multiple failures common.
EGR carbon buildup - causes rough idle and reduced performance on city-driven examples.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £425-£800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Z13DT units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Suzuki Wagon R Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Suzuki Wagon R across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0.7L Petrol
entries.history: The K6A powered most JDM Wagon R kei imports, offering 54ps naturally aspirated or 64ps turbo in a compact 3-cylinder package.
compatibleVariants: FX, GL, GLX, SZ, Stingray, Turbo RS (CT/MC/MH21S/MH23S chassis)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear after 100k km on high-revving units
Head gasket failure on higher-mileage examples
Valve lash adjuster noise from oil starvation
entries.cta: Get quotes for K6A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 0.7L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged variant of the K6A, fitted to sporty Stingray and RS models with upgraded internals and IHI turbo.
commonFailures: IHI RHB32 turbo oil coking from restricted feed
Timing chain tensioner wear compounded by turbo heat
Head gasket failure under sustained boost
entries.cta: Get quotes for K6A-T Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 0.7L Petrol
entries.history: Successor to the K6A in sixth-generation MH35S Wagon R, featuring HEARTECT lightweight construction and improved efficiency.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (improved over K6A but still documented)
Carbon buildup on direct injection variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for R06A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early Wagon R+ 1.0L 4-cylinder, simple SOHC design, interference engine requiring timely belt changes.
compatibleVariants: Wagon R+ GL, GLX (early European models)
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage (interference)
Head gasket failure on neglected units
entries.cta: Get quotes for G10A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Updated 1.0L for later Wagon R+, retained timing belt drive, improved emissions and refinement.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference engine) - replace every 60k miles
Head gasket failure on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for K10A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Popular 1.2L 4-cylinder for Wagon R+, good balance of power and economy, timing belt driven.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference) - critical maintenance item
Head gasket failure on neglected or overheated units
entries.cta: Get quotes for K12A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Most popular Wagon R+ engine, timing chain driven, reliable with regular maintenance, excellent parts availability.
compatibleVariants: Wagon R+ GL, GLX, SZ, SZ-L (2000-2008)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear after 100k miles (less critical than K6A)
Head gasket failure on high-mileage or overheated units
entries.cta: Get quotes for M13A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Range-topping 1.6L for Wagon R+, offered strong performance but lower UK sales volume.
compatibleVariants: Wagon R+ SZ, SZ-L (2000-2008, limited availability)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage examples
Head gasket failure less common but documented
entries.cta: Get quotes for M16A Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Diesel
entries.history: Fiat-sourced 1.2 DDiS common-rail diesel, offered strong economy but complex emissions systems and injector sensitivity.
compatibleVariants: Wagon R DDiS GL, GLX, SZ (2003-2014)
commonFailures: Common-rail injector failure (£150-£200 per injector, multiple failures common)
EGR valve carbon buildup causing rough idle and reduced performance
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z13DT Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Wagon R Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Wagon R's dual identity means different engines face different issues: JDM kei 3-cylinders (K6A/R06A) suffer timing chain tensioner wear, while European Wagon R+ 4-cylinders (K10A/K12A/M13A) face timing belt risks, and the Z13DT diesel contends with injector failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your specific Wagon R.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why JDM Kei K6A/R06A Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Wagon R FX, GL, GLX, Stingray, Turbo RS (1998-2012, K6A & K6A-T; 2017-2020, R06A)
problems.rootCause: High-revving 3-cylinder design places stress on the hydraulic tensioner; oil starvation or sludge accelerates wear, causing chain rattle and potential skip.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guide, and chain if stretched; oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before chain skip; 30-50k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K6A/R06A with upgraded tensioner kit, MLS head gasket, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: 12-month warranty; addresses root cause; best value on 80k+ mile units

problems.recommendation: On a JDM kei Wagon R worth £1,500-£3,000 with over 80k miles, replacement is often more cost-effective than preventative repair. A rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner eliminates recurrence risk and comes with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A engine prices for your Suzuki Wagon R

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Wagon R+ K10A/K12A Engines Suffer Catastrophic Damage
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Wagon R+ GL, GLX, SZ (1997-2000, G10A/K10A/K12A)
problems.rootCause: These are interference engines; a snapped timing belt causes pistons to strike open valves, bending valves and potentially damaging pistons and cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, and water pump; set timing correctly
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance every 60k miles; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, reface seats, new head gasket; only viable if block undamaged
repairOptions.longevity: Risk of hidden damage; suitable only if belt snapped at low RPM with minimal piston contact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K10A/K12A with new belt kit, MLS gasket, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution on high-mileage units; 12-month warranty; avoids hidden damage risk

problems.recommendation: If the belt has snapped on a K10A/K12A Wagon R+, replacement is almost always better value than cylinder head repair. Hidden damage is common, and a rebuilt unit with new belt kit provides warranty peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K12A Suzuki Wagon R+ engine replacement

problems.h4: Common-Rail Injector Failure - Why Z13DT Diesel Wagon R Owners Face Costly Repairs
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Wagon R DDiS GL, GLX, SZ (2003-2014, Z13DT)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common-rail system is sensitive to fuel contamination; injector nozzles wear, causing misfires, rough idle, and increased emissions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only one injector failed and vehicle under 100k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, code to ECU, flush fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Costly; often approaches replacement cost on 100k+ mile units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z13DT with tested/renewed injectors, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value on high-mileage diesel Wagon R; avoids repeated injector bills

problems.recommendation: On a Z13DT Wagon R with over 100k miles and multiple injector failures, replacement is more economical than repeated injector repairs. A rebuilt unit with tested injectors provides warranty protection and eliminates recurrence risk.
problems.cta: Compare Z13DT diesel engine prices for your Suzuki Wagon R

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - A Risk Across All Suzuki Wagon R Petrol Engines
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Wagon R FX, GL, GLX, Stingray, Wagon R+ GL, GLX, SZ (1997-2012, K6A/K6A-T/K10A/K12A/M13A)
problems.rootCause: Overheating, poor coolant maintenance, or age causes gasket degradation; coolant enters combustion chamber or oil passages.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block and head undamaged; requires skilled labour; no warranty on recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with MLS head gasket upgrade, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value on high-mileage units; MLS gasket reduces recurrence risk; warranty included

problems.recommendation: If your Wagon R has over 100k miles and the head gasket has failed, replacement is often more cost-effective than repair. A rebuilt unit with MLS gasket upgrade provides warranty protection and addresses the root cause.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Suzuki Wagon R engine with MLS gasket upgrade

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why K6A-T Stingray/RS Owners Face Oil Coking Issues
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Wagon R Stingray, Turbo RS (2008-2012, K6A-T)
problems.rootCause: IHI RHB32 turbo suffers oil coking from restricted feed line; heat soak after hard driving accelerates carbon buildup, causing bearing wear and boost loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Rebuild with Upgraded Oil Feed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, clean/replace bearings, fit upgraded oil feed restrictor, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block undamaged; upgraded feed reduces recurrence risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K6A-T with upgraded turbo oil feed, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value on high-mileage Stingray/RS; addresses turbo and engine wear; warranty included

problems.recommendation: On a K6A-T Wagon R with turbo failure and over 70k miles, replacement is often better value than turbo rebuild alone. A rebuilt unit with upgraded oil feed eliminates recurrence risk and comes with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A-T turbo engine prices for your Suzuki Wagon R Stingray

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Wagon R
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you own a JDM kei Wagon R with K6A timing chain concerns, a European Wagon R+ with timing belt risks, or a diesel DDiS facing injector failures, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Suzuki Wagon R specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Wagon R Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Wagon R and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

groups.title: Suzuki Wagon R Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R 0.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 0.7L three-cylinder petrol engine powered all JDM kei Wagon R imports (CT/MC/MH chassis), offering compact size and high-revving character. Available naturally aspirated (54ps) or turbocharged (64ps) in Stingray/RS variants, these engines use timing chains and require specialist knowledge for replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear after 100k km; head gasket failure on high-mileage units; turbo oil coking on K6A-T
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Wagon R 0.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L four-cylinder petrol engine was fitted to early European Wagon R+ models (1997-2000), offering simple SOHC design and good economy. These interference engines use timing belts, making belt replacement critical to avoid catastrophic valve damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causes bent valves (interference); head gasket failure on neglected units
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Wagon R 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L four-cylinder petrol engine powered later Wagon R+ models, offering improved power and refinement over the 1.0L. Like the 1.0L, these are interference engines with timing belts, requiring strict maintenance intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure (interference engine); head gasket failure on overheated units
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Wagon R 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L four-cylinder petrol engine was the most popular choice for European Wagon R+ (2000-2008), featuring timing chain drive and excellent reliability with regular maintenance. Strong parts availability makes this a cost-effective replacement option.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear after 100k miles; head gasket failure on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Wagon R 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L four-cylinder petrol engine was the range-topping option for Wagon R+, offering strong performance but limited UK availability. Timing chain driven and generally reliable, but higher replacement cost reflects lower production volumes.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage units; less common but documented head gasket issues
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Wagon R 1.6L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Suzuki Wagon R Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R 1.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.2L DDiS diesel engine was offered on European Wagon R+ from 2003-2014, providing strong fuel economy but complex common-rail injection. Injector sensitivity and DPF issues on short-journey vehicles are key considerations for replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Common-rail injector failure (£150-£200 per injector); EGR carbon buildup; DPF regeneration issues
items.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Wagon R 1.2L diesel engines

fuelTypes.h2: Suzuki Wagon R Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Suzuki Wagon R engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early JDM kei 0.7L petrol to the later Wagon R+ 1.3L and 1.2L diesel.

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines dominate the Wagon R range, with JDM kei 0.7L three-cylinders (K6A/R06A) and European Wagon R+ 1.0-1.6L four-cylinders (G10A/K10A/K12A/M13A/M16A) covering all generations.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominate the Wagon R range, with JDM kei 0.7L three-cylinders (K6A/R06A) and European Wagon R+ 1.0-1.6L four-cylinders (G10A/K10A/K12A/M13A/M16A) covering all generations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Suzuki Wagon R Petrol Engine Families
families: K6A/K6A-T - JDM kei 0.7L three-cylinder, timing chain, high-revving, tensioner wear common
R06A - Sixth-gen JDM kei 0.7L, HEARTECT platform, improved tensioner design
M13A - Wagon R+ 1.3L four-cylinder, timing chain, most popular European engine
K12A/K10A - Early Wagon R+ 1.0/1.2L, timing belt interference engines
foundIn: JDM Kei (CT/MC/MH chassis) (1993-2020) - F6A, K6A, K6A-T, R06A
European Wagon R+ (1997-2008) - G10A, K10A, K12A, M13A, M16A
knownFor: K6A/R06A timing chain tensioner wear after 100k km on high-revving three-cylinders
K10A/K12A timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage (interference engines)
M13A generally reliable with regular maintenance; strong parts availability in UK
Head gasket failure documented across all petrol variants on high-mileage units
typicalModels: Wagon R+ GL, GLX, SZ, SZ-L (European)
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Wagon R Petrol engine prices

items.title: Suzuki Wagon R Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.2L DDiS diesel (Z13DT) was offered on European Wagon R+ from 2003-2014, providing strong economy but complex common-rail injection requiring specialist knowledge.
items.descriptor: The 1.2L DDiS diesel (Z13DT) was offered on European Wagon R+ from 2003-2014, providing strong economy but complex common-rail injection requiring specialist knowledge.
items.familiesLabel: Common Suzuki Wagon R Diesel Engine Families
families: Z13DT - Fiat-sourced 1.2L common-rail diesel, injector sensitivity and EGR issues documented
knownFor: Common-rail injector failure (£150-£200 per injector) is the most common replacement driver
EGR carbon buildup causes rough idle and reduced performance on city-driven examples
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey vehicles; replacement often more cost-effective than repeated repairs
Diesel production ended 2014; injector availability can be limited on high-mileage units
typicalModels: Wagon R DDiS GL, GLX, SZ
items.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Wagon R Diesel replacement engines

engineYears.h2: Suzuki Wagon R Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Suzuki Wagon R's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Wagon R launched in Japan with the F6A 657cc three-cylinder engine, establishing the kei car formula.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation Wagon R (CT21S chassis) in Japan
F6A 657cc SOHC three-cylinder petrol engine only
Carburetted and MPFI variants; 55ps output
knownFor: Simple, lightweight design ideal for urban use
Carburettor variants require regular tuning
Rare in UK; mostly grey imports with specialist support needed
years.cta: Get quotes for 1993-1998 Suzuki Wagon R replacement engines

years.description: The K6A engine replaced the F6A, introducing DOHC design and improved efficiency for the second-generation kei Wagon R.
keyChanges: Introduction of K6A 658cc DOHC three-cylinder engine
Naturally aspirated (54ps) and turbo (64ps) variants available
MC21S chassis with updated styling and interior
mainEngines: K6A - 0.7L petrol naturally aspirated (FX, GL, GLX)
knownFor: K6A timing chain tensioner wear emerges as common issue after 100k km
Turbo variants (K6A-T) suffer IHI turbo oil coking without upgraded feed
Head gasket failure documented on high-mileage examples
years.cta: Compare K6A Suzuki Wagon R engine prices for your generation
years.ticker: Suzuki Wagon R K6A engine replacement • K6A timing chain tensioner failure • Suzuki Wagon R turbo engine repair
enquiries: Suzuki Wagon R K6A engine replacement
Suzuki Wagon R turbo engine repair

years.description: Suzuki launched the wider-body Wagon R+ for Europe, featuring four-cylinder engines completely different from JDM kei models.
keyChanges: European-market Wagon R+ launched with 1.0L and 1.2L four-cylinder engines
Wider body, five-door configuration, right-hand drive for UK
Timing belt-driven interference engines (critical maintenance item)
mainEngines: K12A - 1.2L petrol (most popular early Wagon R+)
knownFor: Timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage (interference engines)
Head gasket failure on neglected or overheated units
Strong parts availability in UK; affordable replacement options from £325
years.cta: Get quotes for Wagon R+ 1997-2000 replacement engines
years.ticker: Wagon R+ timing belt replacement • K12A engine failure • Suzuki Wagon R+ engine cost

years.description: The M13A 1.3L timing-chain engine became the dominant Wagon R+ powerplant, offering improved reliability and strong UK parts support.
keyChanges: M13A 1.3L timing-chain engine introduced, replacing belt-driven units
M16A 1.6L optional on higher trims (limited UK availability)
Updated emissions and refinement for Euro 4 compliance
mainEngines: M13A - 1.3L petrol timing chain (GL, GLX, SZ, SZ-L)
knownFor: M13A widely regarded as most reliable Wagon R+ engine with regular maintenance
Timing chain tensioner wear documented after 100k miles but less critical than K6A
Head gasket failure less common but still occurs on high-mileage units
years.cta: Compare M13A Suzuki Wagon R+ engine prices
years.ticker: M13A engine replacement • Wagon R+ 1.3 engine cost • Suzuki Wagon R+ reliable engine

years.description: The Z13DT 1.2L common-rail diesel was offered on European Wagon R+, providing strong economy but complex injection system sensitivity.
keyChanges: Z13DT 1.2L DDiS diesel introduced for Wagon R+ (2003)
Common-rail injection, turbocharging, and later DPF for Euro 5
Diesel production ended 2014 as focus shifted to petrol hybrids
mainEngines: Z13DT - 1.2L diesel common-rail turbo (DDiS GL, GLX, SZ)
knownFor: Common-rail injector failure is the dominant replacement driver (£150-£200 per injector)
EGR carbon buildup and DPF issues on short-journey vehicles
Replacement often more cost-effective than repeated injector repairs on 100k+ mile units
years.cta: Get quotes for Z13DT Suzuki Wagon R diesel engine replacement
years.ticker: Z13DT injector failure • Wagon R diesel engine replacement • Suzuki DDiS engine cost

years.description: The MH21S generation refined the K6A engine with improved emissions and CVT option, maintaining the kei car formula.
keyChanges: MH21S chassis with updated styling and interior
K6A engine refined for lower emissions; CVT automatic option introduced
Turbo variants continued with IHI RHB32 turbo
mainEngines: K6A - 0.7L petrol naturally aspirated (FX, GL, GLX)
knownFor: K6A timing chain tensioner wear remains the dominant failure pattern
CVT compatibility essential when sourcing replacement engines
Turbo variants require upgraded oil feed to prevent recurrence
years.cta: Compare MH21S Suzuki Wagon R engine prices

years.description: The MH23S generation introduced the Stingray sport variant with K6A-T turbo, while refining the base K6A for improved efficiency.
keyChanges: MH23S chassis with sharper styling and improved interior
Stingray variant launched with sporty body kit and K6A-T turbo
Refined K6A with improved emissions for Japanese regulations
mainEngines: K6A - 0.7L petrol naturally aspirated (FX, GL, GLX)
knownFor: K6A-T turbo oil coking from restricted feed is the dominant failure on Stingray/RS
Base K6A tensioner wear still common after 100k km
Enthusiast demand for Stingray keeps replacement parts available
years.cta: Get quotes for MH23S Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement
years.ticker: Stingray turbo engine replacement • K6A-T oil coking fix • Suzuki Wagon R RS engine cost
enquiries: Suzuki Wagon R RS engine cost

years.description: The MH35S generation introduced the R06A engine with HEARTECT lightweight platform and optional mild-hybrid assistance.
keyChanges: MH35S chassis with HEARTECT lightweight construction
R06A engine replaced K6A, featuring improved efficiency and emissions
Optional mild-hybrid (R06D) with 12V belt-driven starter-generator
mainEngines: R06A - 0.7L petrol naturally aspirated (FX, GL, GLX)
knownFor: R06A timing chain tensioner improved over K6A but still documented after 100k km
Mild-hybrid R06D requires specialist knowledge for replacement; battery inspection recommended
Lower UK import volumes mean fewer replacement options; plan ahead

faq.h2: Suzuki Wagon R Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Suzuki Wagon R?
items.answer: Yes - Wagon R engines are affordable to replace. JDM kei units from £400, Wagon R+ from £325, making replacement often cheaper than major repairs on high-mileage cars.
keyPoints: Rebuilt K6A engines from £500; M13A from £325
Replacement avoids hidden damage risk from belt/chain failure
12-month warranty included on rebuilt units through EngineMarket
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Suzuki Wagon R engine prices for your variant

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Suzuki Wagon R engine in the UK?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines range from £325 (M13A Wagon R+) to £1,100 (K6A-T turbo). Supply & fit adds £400-£800 depending on garage location and engine complexity.
keyPoints: Wagon R+ M13A: £325-£600 supply only
JDM kei K6A: £500-£1,100 supply only
Z13DT diesel: £425-£800 supply only; injector testing included
items.cta: Get a tailored Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement quote today

items.question: Can I fit a Wagon R+ engine to my JDM kei Wagon R?
items.answer: No - JDM kei (3-cylinder 658cc) and European Wagon R+ (4-cylinder 1.0-1.6L) engines are completely different. Mounts, ECUs, and wiring are not interchangeable.
keyPoints: JDM kei uses F6A/K6A/R06A three-cylinder engines
Wagon R+ uses G10A/K10A/K12A/M13A four-cylinder engines
Chassis codes CT/MC/MH = JDM kei; Wagon R+ badge = European
items.cta: Identify your exact Wagon R generation before ordering a replacement engine

items.question: What is the difference between Suzuki Wagon R and Wagon R+?
items.answer: Wagon R (JDM kei) has 3-cylinder 658cc engines (K6A/F6A/R06A). Wagon R+ (European) has 4-cylinder 1.0-1.6L engines (G10A/K10A/K12A/M13A). Completely different vehicles - engines are NOT interchangeable.
keyPoints: JDM kei: narrow body, 3-door, 658cc three-cylinder engines
Wagon R+: wide body, 5-door, 1.0-1.6L four-cylinder engines
Check chassis code (CT/MC/MH = JDM) or badge (Wagon R+ = European)
items.cta: Use our registration lookup to confirm your exact Wagon R variant

items.question: Which Suzuki Wagon R engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The M13A 1.3L petrol in European Wagon R+ (2000-2008) is widely regarded as the most reliable, with timing chain drive and strong UK parts availability.
keyPoints: M13A timing chain more durable than K6A tensioner-prone design
Regular oil changes critical for all Wagon R engines
Avoid early K10A/K12A if timing belt history is unknown
items.cta: Compare M13A Suzuki Wagon R+ engine prices for reliable replacement

items.question: How do I find my Suzuki Wagon R engine code and generation?
items.answer: Check the chassis plate (CT/MC/MH = JDM kei 3-cylinder; Wagon R+ badge = European 4-cylinder). Engine code is stamped on the block near the oil filter or on the chassis VIN plate.
keyPoints: JDM kei codes: F6A, K6A, K6A-T, R06A (0.7L three-cylinder)
Wagon R+ codes: G10A, K10A, K12A, M13A, M16A, Z13DT (1.0-1.6L four-cylinder)
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA verification
items.cta: Get instant engine code identification with our free registration lookup

items.question: Do you supply and fit Suzuki Wagon R engines?
items.answer: Yes - UK-wide supply & fit is available through our specialist network, with garages experienced in JDM kei imports and European Wagon R+ variants.
keyPoints: Fitting cost typically £400-£800 added to engine price
CVT and 4WD specialists available where required
Warranty covers both parts and labour when using network garages
items.cta: Find Suzuki Wagon R engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Suzuki Wagon R replacement engine?
items.answer: 12-24 months unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt and reconditioned units supplied through EngineMarket, covering parts and labour at network garages.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt engines
Up to 24 months available on remanufactured units
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across Suzuki Wagon R engine suppliers

items.question: How long does a Suzuki Wagon R engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch in 1-3 working days. Supply & fit takes 2-4 days depending on garage availability and engine complexity (CVT/4WD variants take longer).
keyPoints: JDM kei engines may require additional ECU adaptation time
Wagon R+ engines generally quicker to fit due to UK parts familiarity
Book garage slot in advance to minimise vehicle downtime
items.cta: Get quotes for fast-dispatch Suzuki Wagon R replacement engines

items.question: Will a JDM Suzuki Wagon R engine work in my UK-registered car?
items.answer: Only if your UK car is also a JDM kei import (CT/MC/MH chassis). JDM 3-cylinder engines are NOT compatible with European Wagon R+ 4-cylinder models - completely different mounting and electronics.
keyPoints: Verify chassis code before ordering: CT/MC/MH = JDM kei compatible
JDM engines may require ECU adaptation for UK emissions compliance
Use our registration lookup to confirm compatibility instantly
items.cta: Confirm your Wagon R generation before ordering a replacement engine
